---
title: "Lab: Arithmetic in R"
date: 2026-05-24
description: '<a href="rlab-downloads/arithmetic.qmd" download="arithmetic.qmd">Download .qmd</a>'
format: html
---

## Purpose

R can be used like a calculator. This lab walks through the four basic arithmetic operations — addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division — so you can confirm your R environment is working and get comfortable running code chunks.

## Addition

Use `+` to add two numbers:

```{r}
2 + 3
```

## Subtraction

Use `-` to subtract:

```{r}
10 - 4
```

## Multiplication

Use `*` to multiply:

```{r}
6 * 7
```

## Division

Use `/` to divide:

```{r}
20 / 5
```

## Try it yourself

In a new code chunk (or directly in the R console), compute the following:

- Add 15 and 27
- Subtract 9 from 50
- Multiply 8 by 12
- Divide 144 by 12

```{r}
# your code here
```
